brief.pull_quote: The test is this: after you close the laptop, is work still happening? If yes, you have an execution layer. If no, you have an expensive chatbot.
brief.angle: Most solopreneurs stop at the reasoning model — the real leverage is the 3 layers stacked on top of it.
brief.title_draft: The 4-Layer AI Solopreneur Stack (and why most stop at Layer 1)

post.post_text:
18 months and 12 client projects later, I finally have a stack I defend.

Most AI solopreneur advice is a tool dump. Thirty apps, a subscription list, good luck.
That is not a stack. That is a credit card statement.

A real stack has 4 layers. Reasoning. Execution. Memory. Distribution.
Each layer does one job the layer above it cannot do for itself.

The test is this: after you close the laptop, is work still happening? If yes, you have an execution layer. If no, you have an expensive chatbot.

Most solopreneurs stop at layer one. They pay for a reasoning model, then burn afternoons copy-pasting into twelve browser tabs.

Layer two is where the real work hides: agents that actually touch your repo, your CRM, your invoicing — without you in the loop.

Layer three is the one nobody talks about until it breaks. Memory. Every session starting from zero context is a tax you pay every morning.

Layer four is distribution. The unsexy one. Nobody tweets about their distribution pipeline, but layers one to three without it are just an expensive hobby.

When I onboard another solopreneur, I never ask which tools they use.
I ask which layer breaks first when they try to scale past themselves.

Which one is breaking first for you right now?
post.link_comment: The onboarding test I run on every solopreneur client — and the 4 failure modes I have seen in the wild: https://alisadikinma.com/blog/ai-solopreneur-stack
